title: old() on non-Copy types silently requires using(allocator) — invisible coupling
description: When an ensures clause uses old(expr) and expr is a slice or pointer-containing type, the compiler needs to allocate a snapshot. This requires an ambient allocator. The connection is invisible: the ensures clause says old(items), not old(items) using allocator. A function that adds old() to an ensures clause may now silently require that the caller wrap it in a with block where it did not before. This is a hidden API-breaking change. What caller observes: compile error about missing ambient allocator, caused by a change in the callee's ensures clause.
